frame_analyses.description: First performance attempt - trainer view showing peg board with 6 colored spheres (red, purple, dark purple), right-hand grasper visible, timer showing 0 drops, 1 correct placement, 3.92 seconds elapsed

frame_analyses.description: Grasper manipulating purple sphere, positioning for transfer. Timer shows 1 placement completed, elapsed ~3.22 seconds
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth grasp technique, deliberate instrument movement, minimal wasted motion

frame_analyses.description: Performance completion screen showing 'Pick Up & Place' task passed with: Time 6.78s (Max nominal 17s), Mistakes 0, Right pathlength 10.64 (Max 21)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completed successfully. Time well under proficient threshold (6.78s vs 17s nominal max)

frame_analyses.description: Leaderboard showing multiple attempts by Maurice Zwart with scores: first attempt 9.67 (6.28 performance score), second attempt 10.07 (6.4 score), third attempt 6.78 (from completion screen), ranking 1st overall
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Performance data showing progressive improvement across attempts and consistent high proficiency

frame_analyses.description: Final leaderboard snapshot showing Maurice Zwart with completion times: 10.07s (6.4 score), Thomas van Doeveren 14.25s (7 score), confirming top ranking with fastest completion
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Expert-level performance confirmed by scoring system and leaderboard ranking

video_type_determination: This video is primarily INSTRUCTIONAL content showing:
instructional_elements: Title slide with instructor names (Maurice Zwart, Jaap Scholten)
Multiple performance attempts by same trainee over time
scoring_decision: DO NOT SCORE - This is an instructional/educational video showcasing the SIMENDO platform, training progression, and learning management system rather than a single focused performance evaluation for FLS scoring purposes.

demonstrated_techniques: Efficient right-hand grasping with proper instrument control
Smooth peg transfer mechanics with minimal wasted motion
Consistent placement accuracy across multiple attempts
Progressive skill improvement through repeated practice attempts
verbal_cues: Instructional context: SIMENDO training platform overview
Performance metrics displayed: Time, Mistakes, Pathlength
common_errors_addressed: Video demonstrates expert-level performance avoiding common drop penalties
Shows importance of consistent technique across multiple attempts
Illustrates competitive benchmarking and proficiency thresholds
teaching_content.evidence: Maurice Zwart achieves 6.78s completion time (6.4 SIMENDO score) on final visible attempt, ranking #1 on leaderboard, demonstrating expert proficiency well above the 40-60s expert benchmark for traditional FLS peg transfer (note: SIMENDO timing may use different scale)

confidence_rationale: Video classification as instructional is definitive based on: (1) explicit title slide crediting instructors, (2) systematic progression through platform features, (3) educational dashboard overlays, (4) multiple performance attempts shown with leaderboard context, (5) no single continuous scored performance attempt from start to finish. While performance data is embedded, the primary narrative is teaching/demonstrating the SIMENDO system rather than scoring a single trainee performance.
cannot_determine: Whether any individual embedded performance attempt should be scored independently - video context is training/learning system overview rather than formal evaluation
summary: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL video showcasing the SIMENDO laparoscopic training platform, demonstrating its features, training progression tracking, and competitive leaderboard functionality. While it contains embedded performance data showing Maurice Zwart's peg transfer attempts (achieving expert-level times of 6-10 seconds across multiple tries), the primary pedagogical intent is to educate viewers about the platform capabilities and training methodology rather than to present a single formal FLS performance for scoring. Per scoring guidelines, instructional content with narration, overlays, and educational framing should not be scored as performance; teaching content should be extracted instead.
